chunks.text: Bees are one of the most important instrument for humanity.
They fly from flower to flower, carrying pollen.
Without this work, many crops do not bear fruit.
One - third of the food grown in the world depends on pollinators such as bees.
The richness of our table is supported by the work of small bees.
But in recent years, the number of bees has been decreasing around the world.
The causes are complex.
Pesticides, the destruction of habitat, climate change, parasites.
Especially serious are pesticides whose ingredient includes neonicotinoids.
These chemicals interfere with the nervous system of bees and cause them to lose their sense of direction.
Bees that cannot return to the hive are isolated and eventually die.
The EU has banned the use of this pesticide.
Other countries are gradually strengthening restrict.
But there is also strong opposition to regulation.
Farmers' yields fall.
What must be weighed is profit against the environment.
Citizens can also act.
Plant plenty of flowers in the garden and provide bees with nectar.
Buy honey from local beekeepers.
Try gardening without pesticides.
Bees operate within the circuit of the ecosystem.
If they disappear, many organisms will be affected in sequence.
In the end, the food security of humanity itself comes under threat.
A small being supports a great world.
To protect bees is to protect our own future.
